title: Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America & the Caribbean
summary: Applications are now officially open for the prestigious Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) 2027 at Georgetown University in Washington, D.C. This highly competitive and transformative leadership development program is designed for emerging leaders across Latin America and the Caribbean who are already creating meaningful change within their communities and organizations. Through a powerful combination of virtual learning, mentorship, leadership development, and in-person academic engagement at Georgetown University, participants will gain the tools, networks, and strategic vision needed to expand their social impact across the region. The Global Competitiveness Leadership (GCL) Program is hosted by Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program (LALP) and is recognized as one of the region’s most influential leadership experiences for young changemakers. The 2027 cohort will run from January 11 to March 19, 2027, combining online sessions with an intensive in-person experience in Washington, D.C. Application Deadline Applications for the GCL 2027 Program are open from: Opening Date: June 15, 2026 Deadline: August 14, 2026 (11:59 PM EDT) Selected applicants will receive admission notifications by November 2, 2026. About the Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program is an academic and experiential leadership initiative focused on developing the next generation of influential leaders capable of promoting competitiveness, innovation, sustainable development, and social transformation throughout Latin America and the Caribbean. The program supports leaders who are already working on projects with long-term social impact and seeks to amplify their effectiveness through advanced leadership training, collaboration, and regional networking opportunities. Participants become part of a strong network of changemakers, innovators, and professionals committed to driving positive transformation within their countries and communities. Program Structure and Duration The GCL 2027 Program is a 10-week hybrid experience that combines virtual engagement with immersive in-person learning at Georgetown University. Program Dates January 11 – March 19, 2027 Virtual Component January 11 – February 12, 2027 Participants will complete four weeks of virtual programming, generally scheduled on: Mondays Wednesdays Selected Fridays Sessions are typically conducted between 9:00 AM and 1:15 PM EST. The virtual component also includes one asynchronous week that allows participants to prepare for travel and continue developing their projects with mentorship support. In-Person Component in Washington, D.C. February 16 – March 19, 2027 Participants will travel to Washington, D.C. for five weeks of intensive in-person programming at Georgetown University. The in-person sessions are conducted: Monday through Friday 9:30 AM – 4:15 PM EST The on-campus experience includes leadership workshops, collaborative projects, mentoring opportunities, academic sessions, networking events, and strategic discussions with regional leaders and experts. Scholarship and Financial Support The total value of the GCL Program is approximately USD $25,000. To make the program more accessible, Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program offers scholarships to admitted participants. During the application process, candidates can request financial assistance by: Indicating that they wish to apply for a scholarship Specifying the scholarship amount requested Explaining their financial need Important Scholarship Information Even participants receiving the maximum scholarship award will still be required to contribute USD $3,500 toward participation costs. What the Program Fee Covers The program fee includes: Ten weeks of virtual and in-person instruction Academic materials and learning resources Continuous mentorship and support throughout the program Shared accommodation during the Washington, D.C. residency Local transportation for official program activities Basic emergency medical insurance during the in-person component Official certificate of participation from Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program Expenses Not Covered Participants are responsible for covering: International airfare Meals Personal expenses Incidentals Any costs not specifically included in the program fee Accommodation Information All admitted participants are required to stay in the accommodation arranged by the program. Housing is provided in shared apartments, usually with: Two participants per room Up to four participants per apartment Please note that family members or guests are not permitted in the program housing. Eligibility Requirements To apply for the GCL 2027 Program, applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 26 and 35 years old by the application deadline. Nationality and Residency Eligible applicants must be citizens and residents of approved countries in Latin America and the Caribbean. Applicants from Spain and Portugal may also apply if their projects positively impact the Latin American region. Educational Qualification Candidates must hold a university degree from an accredited institution and demonstrate a strong academic record. English Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate excellent English proficiency in: Reading Writing Speaking Professional or Volunteer Experience Applicants must have at least one year of professional or volunteer experience related to social impact, leadership, or community development initiatives. Leadership and Impact Requirement Candidates must currently be leading a project that demonstrates positive, sustainable, and long-term impact within their community or region. Commitment to Community Development Participants must commit to returning to their countries or communities after the program to apply the knowledge and skills gained through GCL. Applicants will be required to sign a formal agreement confirming this commitment. Failure to comply with this agreement may require reimbursement of scholarship funds received. Why You Should Apply for GCL 2027 The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program offers much more than academic training. It provides participants with: Advanced leadership development Access to influential regional and global networks Mentorship from experienced professionals Exposure to international perspectives and policy discussions Opportunities to strengthen social impact projects Practical tools for sustainable development and innovation Collaboration with high-performing changemakers from across the region Participants leave the program with stronger leadership capacity, expanded regional influence, and a deeper understanding of how to create scalable impact. Final Thoughts The Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 is a life-changing opportunity for emerging leaders who are passionate about creating lasting social impact across Latin America and the Caribbean. If you are already leading meaningful change in your community and want to strengthen your leadership skills, expand your regional influence, and connect with a powerful network of changemakers, this program could be the next major step in your journey. With scholarships available and access to one of the world’s leading academic institutions, GCL 2027 offers a rare platform for visionary leaders committed to transforming their communities and shaping the future of the region. Learn more and apply Interested candidates must submit their application and all required supporting documents through the official Georgetown University application portal before the deadline. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa
summary: Women’s rights organizations, feminist movements, grassroots collectives, and gender justice activists across Africa now have access to an important funding opportunity through the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Grant Program. This initiative is designed to provide rapid-response and flexible funding to support activists, organizations, and movements responding to urgent threats, emergencies, advocacy opportunities, and critical moments affecting women’s rights and gender justice. The grant program is managed by Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa), a pan-African feminist fund dedicated to supporting women, girls, and gender-diverse individuals working to promote human rights, equality, justice, and social transformation throughout the continent. Unlike traditional funding programs that often involve long waiting periods, this initiative focuses on speed, flexibility, and urgent intervention, allowing activists and movements to respond quickly when immediate action is necessary. About the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Urgent Action Fund–Africa is a feminist and women’s human rights fund that supports courageous activists, grassroots organizations, and movements that are advancing gender justice and protecting vulnerable communities across Africa. The organization prioritizes initiatives that: Protect women human rights defenders Support feminist movement-building Strengthen grassroots organizing Respond to emergencies and crises Advance strategic advocacy opportunities Promote safety, resilience, and wellbeing for activists The fund recognizes that women’s rights defenders and gender justice activists often face threats, violence, political repression, discrimination, and emergency situations that require immediate support and intervention. Through this rapid-response funding mechanism, UAF-Africa helps ensure that activists and organizations can act swiftly during critical moments. What the UAF-Africa Grant Supports The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program provides flexible financial support for urgent situations and opportunities related to women’s rights and gender justice. Selected applicants may receive funding for activities such as: 1. Emergency Response Initiatives Organizations and activists facing urgent threats or crises may receive support to address immediate safety and protection needs. This may include: Emergency relocation Security measures Crisis response activities Protection of activists under threat Legal or advocacy support during emergencies 2. Advocacy and Campaign Activities Funding may support strategic advocacy efforts that advance women’s rights and gender justice. Examples include: Public awareness campaigns Policy advocacy initiatives Human rights mobilization efforts Feminist advocacy campaigns Community organizing activities 3. Movement-Building and Organizing The fund supports initiatives that strengthen feminist movements and grassroots networks. This may include: Coalition-building activities Leadership development Community mobilization Capacity strengthening Networking and collaboration efforts 4. Protection of Women Human Rights Defenders Women human rights defenders often face intimidation, violence, and threats while advocating for justice. The fund prioritizes: Safety and wellbeing initiatives Trauma support Activist protection measures Security planning Safe organizing spaces 5. Strategic Opportunities for Gender Justice The program also supports time-sensitive opportunities that can create meaningful progress for women’s rights and equality. These opportunities may involve: Legislative advocacy windows International advocacy opportunities Community engagement moments Rapid-response mobilization Strategic feminist interventions Benefits of the UAF-Africa Grant Program Applicants selected for the program can benefit from several important forms of support, including: Rapid-response funding for urgent situations Flexible grants tailored to immediate needs Support for advocacy and organizing Assistance for activist protection and safety Funding for feminist movement-building Resources to strengthen resilience and wellbeing Opportunity to respond quickly during critical moments Support for grassroots and informal activist groups One of the key strengths of the program is its flexibility, allowing recipients to use the funding in ways that best respond to their urgent realities and advocacy priorities. Who Can Apply? The opportunity is open to a wide range of applicants working on women’s rights and gender justice issues across Africa. Eligible applicants include: Women’s rights organizations Feminist groups and networks Grassroots collectives Women human rights defenders Gender-diverse activists Community-based organizations Informal activist groups and collectives Women and girls-led initiatives Social justice movements working on gender equality Both registered and informal groups may be eligible, provided they are actively involved in feminist organizing, women’s rights advocacy, or gender justice work. Priority Focus Areas The program prioritizes initiatives working in areas such as: Women’s Rights and Gender Equality Projects focused on advancing equality, inclusion, dignity, and justice for women and marginalized communities. Feminist Organizing and Movement-Building Initiatives that strengthen feminist leadership, solidarity, collaboration, and grassroots mobilization. Protection of Activists Support for organizations and individuals facing threats, harassment, or security challenges while defending human rights. Emergency Response and Crisis Intervention Rapid interventions during emergencies, conflicts, political crises, or urgent human rights situations. Advocacy and Campaigns Strategic campaigns and policy advocacy efforts aimed at advancing gender justice and protecting rights. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on several important factors. These include: Urgency of the situation or opportunity Potential impact on women’s rights and gender justice Strength and relevance of the proposed response Safety and wellbeing considerations for activists Alignment with the mission of UAF-Africa Capacity to implement urgent activities effectively Contribution to feminist movement-building The program places strong emphasis on timely action and meaningful impact. Deadline Deadline: Rolling Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, meaning eligible organizations and activists can apply whenever urgent situations or strategic opportunities arise. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible once an urgent need emerges. Why This Opportunity Matters Across Africa, women human rights defenders and feminist activists continue to play a vital role in promoting justice, equality, and social transformation. However, many activists operate under difficult conditions that include political pressure, insecurity, discrimination, and limited access to rapid funding. The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program helps bridge this gap by providing fast, accessible, and flexible support exactly when it is most needed. This funding opportunity empowers activists and organizations to: Protect vulnerable communities Strengthen feminist movements Respond rapidly to emergencies Advance gender justice initiatives Build resilience and sustainability For organizations and activists working tirelessly to defend human rights and promote equality across Africa, this program represents an important opportunity for timely and impactful support. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs & Women with Disabilities in Uganda
summary: Applications Open for the LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship Program Are you a young woman passionate about science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) and interested in becoming a teacher? The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship offers an incredible opportunity for eligible female students to pursue a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) at selected universities across Uganda with full financial support. The scholarship is designed to increase female participation in STEM teaching, empower future women leaders in education, and address the shortage of qualified female science teachers in Uganda s secondary schools. About the Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program is a five-year initiative funded by the Mastercard Foundation and implemented through a consortium of ten education-focused organizations, including the Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF), UNICEF, British Council, VVOB, Edukans International, Brainwave Careers Uganda, STiR Education, PEAS Uganda, Teach for Uganda, and FENU. Implemented under the leadership of Uganda s Ministry of Education and Sports, the program supports the Mastercard Foundation’s Young Africa Works strategy, which aims to enable millions of young people across Africa to access dignified and fulfilling work by 2030. Through its Recruit, Train, Lead, and Motivate pillars, the program seeks to improve the quality of teaching and learning in secondary schools while expanding opportunities for young women in STEM education. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive comprehensive support throughout their studies, including: Full Tuition and Functional Fees The scholarship covers all tuition and university-related functional fees for the duration of the Bachelor of Science with Education program. Accommodation Support Recipients will receive accommodation support, ensuring a safe and conducive learning environment. Free Laptop Each scholar will receive a laptop to facilitate digital learning, research, and academic success. Scholarship Objectives The scholarship aims to: Increase female participation in STEM teaching careers. Promote equity and inclusion in Uganda s education sector. Expand the supply of qualified female science teachers. Develop female role models who can inspire the next generation of girls in STEM. Target Beneficiaries The program will support: 1,000 female scholars. Young women aged 18–35 years. Refugees and internally displaced persons (7% allocation). Persons with disabilities (5% allocation). Female in-service science, technology, and mathematics teachers seeking to upgrade to a BScEd degree (20% allocation). Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must: 1. Meet the Target Group Requirements Applicants must be: Female Ugandan citizens residing in Uganda; or Female refugees living in Uganda; or Female youth with disabilities living in Uganda. 2. Have Admission to an Eligible Institution Applicants must hold an admission offer for a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) from one of the following institutions: Universities Makerere University Kyambogo University Busitema University Islamic University in Uganda Gulu University Muni University Mountains of the Moon University Mbarara University of Science and Technology Uganda Martyrs University Kabale University UNITE Campuses Kabale Kaliro Mubende Muni Unyama CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE 3. Demonstrate Commitment to Teaching Applicants must show a strong commitment to pursuing a teaching career, particularly in secondary schools, and be willing to serve in rural or underserved communities for at least two years after graduation. 4. Demonstrate Financial Need Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial need and require support to pursue their university education. Why Apply for the LIT Uganda STEM Scholarship? This scholarship goes beyond financial assistance. It provides a pathway for young women to become future STEM educators, leaders, and changemakers who can transform education and inspire girls across Uganda. By investing in female science teachers, the program contributes to stronger education systems, increased gender equality, and improved learning outcomes for future generations. How to Apply Interested and eligible candidates should prepare the required application documents and submit their applications through the official Leaders in Teaching Uganda Program application platform before the stated deadline. CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE Early application is strongly encouraged due to the highly competitive nature of the scholarship. The LIT Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship represents one of the most significant opportunities currently available for young women seeking fully funded university education in Uganda. If you are passionate about STEM subjects, teaching, and creating lasting impact in your community, this scholarship could be your pathway to a rewarding and meaningful career. CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE The post LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs Women with Disabilities in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Paid IOM Internships 2026: International Organization for Migration (IOM) Global Internship Programme – Remote & On-Site Opportunities Worldwide
summary: Applications Open | Paid Internships | Rolling Deadlines | Global Locations Are you looking to gain hands-on experience with a United Nations agency while building a career in international development, migration, humanitarian assistance, public policy, or global affairs? The International Organization for Migration (IOM) , the UN agency dedicated to migration, is currently recruiting interns for multiple paid internship opportunities worldwide. With openings across Europe, Africa, Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East, the IOM Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in a multicultural and international work environment. Whether you are interested in migration management, humanitarian response, artificial intelligence, health programmes, supply chain operations, human resources, or protection programming, there may be an opportunity that matches your interests and career goals. About the IOM Internship Programme The IOM Internship Programme provides talented students and recent graduates with practical exposure to the work of one of the world s leading humanitarian organizations. Interns contribute to ongoing projects while receiving mentorship and professional development opportunities from experienced international staff. Benefits of the Programme Participants may receive: Paid internship opportunities (stipends vary by duty station) Hands-on experience within a United Nations agency Exposure to international migration and humanitarian programming Professional networking opportunities across the UN system Practical skills development in policy, operations, research, and programme management Experience working in diverse and multicultural teams Opportunities to work both remotely and on-site depending on the role Current IOM Internship Opportunities (2026) The following internship positions are currently open: 1. Intern – Canada Visa Application Centre (CVAC) – Full Time (Remunerated) 📍 Almaty, Kazakhstan 📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026 2. Intern – Health Assessment Programmes 📍 Athens, Greece 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 3. Intern – Protection 📍 Pretoria, South Africa 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 4. Intern – Human Resources 📍 Panama City, Panama 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 5. Intern – Programme and Liaison Support 📍 Brussels, Belgium 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 6. Intern 📍 Bern, Switzerland 📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026 7. Intern – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions and Operational Support 📍 Quito, Ecuador 📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026 8. Intern – Supply Chain 📍 Dakar, Senegal 📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026 9. Intern (CinemArena Project) 📍 Rome, Italy 📅 Deadline: 25 June 2026 10. Internship 📍 New Delhi, India 📅 Deadline: 24 June 2026 Additional internship opportunities may be added regularly through IOM s recruitment platform. Candidates are encouraged to monitor the vacancies page frequently. Internship Duration Typical internship length: 2–6 months Extensions may be granted up to 9 months Some duty stations may prefer candidates available for longer placements Eligibility Criteria IOM welcomes applications from highly motivated individuals who are passionate about migration, development, humanitarian action, and international cooperation. Ideal candidates include: Undergraduate students Graduate students Recent graduates Young professionals seeking international experience Candidates from diverse academic disciplines Relevant fields of study include: International Relations Development Studies Political Science Public Policy Migration Studies Human Rights International Law Public Health Supply Chain Management Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Business Administration Communications and Media Studies Why Consider an IOM Internship? Interning with IOM offers much more than work experience. Participants gain first-hand exposure to global migration challenges and solutions while contributing to programmes that impact millions of people worldwide. Interns often support initiatives related to: Refugee and migrant protection Humanitarian assistance Emergency response Health assessments Labour migration Border management Counter-trafficking efforts Data and research Artificial intelligence and digital transformation Policy development and advocacy The experience can also serve as a strong pathway toward future careers within the United Nations system, international NGOs, donor agencies, and development organizations. How to Apply for IOM Internships Step 1: Create a New IOM Recruitment Account IOM recently launched a new e-recruitment platform. If you had an account on the previous recruitment system, you must create a new account before applying for future vacancies. To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1 selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 Important: Failure to update your profile may result in automatic disqualification from the recruitment process. Step 2: Complete Your Candidate Profile Click Apply Now on your chosen vacancy Create your candidate profile Upload your CV and supporting documents Complete all required sections carefully Step 3: Submit Your Application Review your application thoroughly before submission and ensure all information is accurate and up to date. Application Tips To strengthen your application: Highlight relevant academic coursework Include volunteer or professional experience related to migration or humanitarian work Demonstrate cross-cultural experience and international exposure Showcase research, analytical, communication, or project management skills Tailor your application to the specific internship position If your internship is linked to university requirements or supported by a scholarship programme, mention this clearly in your cover letter. Beware of Recruitment Scams IOM has issued an important warning regarding fraudulent recruitment activities. Remember: IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process. IOM will never request bank account information or passwords . Official communications come only from email addresses ending in @iom.int . If you receive suspicious messages claiming to represent IOM, report them immediately via: 📧 talentpool@iom.int Key Details at a Glance Category Details Organization International Organization for Migration (IOM) Programme Global Internship Programme 2026 Funding Paid (stipend varies by location) Duration 2–9 Months Locations Worldwide Eligibility Students Recent Graduates Application Deadline Rolling (varies by position) Work Format On-site and Selected Remote Opportunities Final Thoughts The IOM Paid Internship Programme 2026 is one of the most valuable international internship opportunities currently available for students and recent graduates interested in migration, humanitarian action, development, public policy, and global affairs. With paid placements across multiple continents, opportunities to work alongside international experts, and exposure to real-world humanitarian challenges, the programme provides an excellent platform for launching a career within the United Nations system and the broader international development sector. Given the competitive nature of IOM internships and the rolling recruitment process, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Apply today and take the next step toward an impactful global career with IOM. To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1 selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 Find out more and Start your journey with IOM today. Apply now. title: Google Student Researcher Program 2026: Applications Open for Bachelor’s, Master’s and PhD Students Worldwide
summary: Are you looking for a prestigious research internship at one of the world s leading technology companies? Applications are now open for the Google Student Researcher Program 2026 , offering exciting opportunities for Bachelor s, Master s, and PhD students to work alongside Google s research and engineering teams. The Google Student Researcher Program provides students with the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research projects while gaining hands-on experience in some of the most innovative areas of computer science and technology. About the Google Student Researcher Program 2026 The Student Researcher Program is designed for students pursuing undergraduate, master s, and doctoral degrees who are interested in advancing research and innovation in technology. Participants will collaborate with Google researchers and engineers on impactful projects that address real-world challenges. Available Positions 1. Student Researcher, BS/MS, Winter/Summer 2026 Locations: United States (Mountain View, California; Ann Arbor, Michigan; and multiple additional locations) Canada (Waterloo, Ontario; Montreal, Quebec; and other locations) Eligibility: Currently enrolled in a Bachelor s or Master s degree program in: Computer Science Linguistics Statistics Biostatistics Applied Mathematics Operations Research Economics Natural Sciences Related technical fields 2. Student Researcher, PhD, Winter/Summer 2026 Locations: United States (Mountain View, California; Ann Arbor, Michigan; and multiple additional locations) Canada (Waterloo, Ontario; Montreal, Quebec; and other locations) Eligibility: Currently enrolled in a PhD program in a relevant technical discipline. 3. Student Researcher, 2026 (India) Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India Eligibility: Currently enrolled in a Bachelor s, Master s, or PhD degree program in relevant technical fields. Research Areas Successful applicants may work in one or more of the following areas: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Machine Learning Deep Learning Natural Language Understanding Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) Computer Vision Data Science Software Engineering Quantum Information Science Algorithmic Foundations of Optimization Related computer science and technology disciplines Why Apply? Participants in the Google Student Researcher Program can: Work with world-class researchers and engineers Gain hands-on research experience at Google Contribute to innovative technology projects Build professional networks within the tech industry Strengthen academic and career prospects Develop skills in advanced research and emerging technologies CLICK HERE TO Visit Google s official careers portal to review position details, eligibility requirements, available locations, and application instructions. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for students passionate about research, innovation, artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, software engineering, and other advanced computing fields. Students with prior experience in research projects, programming, data analysis, or related technical work are encouraged to apply. Application Deadline Applications are reviewed according to the requirements and timelines specified for each position. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. How to Apply CLICK HERE TO Visit Google s official careers portal to review position details, eligibility requirements, available locations, and application instructions. title: African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) Jobs 2026: Multiple Vacancies Open in South Africa and Rwanda
summary: AUDA-NEPAD Recruitment 2026: Apply for Finance, Procurement, Programme and Project Management Jobs Are you looking for an opportunity to work with a leading Pan-African institution committed to advancing Africa s development agenda? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) has announced several exciting job openings for qualified professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development across the continent. These AUDA-NEPAD vacancies offer the chance to work in key areas such as finance, procurement, water and sanitation, programme implementation, and project management while supporting impactful initiatives that place youth and women at the center of development. Why Work with AUDA-NEPAD? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) is the development agency of the African Union, dedicated to promoting regional integration, economic growth, good governance, and sustainable development across Africa. Working with AUDA-NEPAD provides professionals with the opportunity to make meaningful contributions to Africa s transformation while collaborating with experts from across the continent. AUDA-NEPAD Job Vacancies 2026 1. Finance Officer (P2) Deadline: 22 June 2026 Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa Supervisor: Head of Finance 2. Finance Assistant (GSA5) Deadline: 22 June 2026 Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa Supervisor: Finance Officer, Grants 3. Head of Project Delivery Office (P4 Step 5) Deadline: 26 June 2026 Duty Station: Kigali, Rwanda Supervisor: Chief of Staff 4. Programme and Implementation Officer (P2 Step 5) Deadline: 26 June 2026 Duty Station: Kigali, Rwanda Supervisor: Head of Project Delivery Office 5. Senior Programme Officer, Water and Sanitation (P3) Deadline: 29 June 2026 Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa Supervisor: Project Coordinator – WASH Project 6. Procurement Officer (P2) Deadline: 29 June 2026 Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa Supervisor: Senior Procurement Officer Who Should Apply? These opportunities are ideal for professionals with backgrounds in: Finance and Accounting Procurement and Supply Chain Management Project Management Programme Implementation Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) Public Health and Medicines Regulation International Development Public Administration Candidates with relevant qualifications and experience who are passionate about Africa s development are encouraged to apply. How to Apply for AUDA-NEPAD Jobs Interested applicants can access detailed job descriptions, eligibility requirements, and application instructions through the official AUDA-NEPAD careers portal. Apply Here: https://www.nepad.org/work-with-us/careers Applicants experiencing technical challenges during registration or application submission may contact: audahiring@nepad.org Important Application Deadlines Position Application Deadline Finance Officer (P2) 22 June 2026 Finance Assistant (GSA5) 22 June 2026 Head of Project Delivery Office (P4 Step 5) 26 June 2026 Programme and Implementation Officer (P2 Step 5) 26 June 2026 Senior Programme Officer, Water and Sanitation (P3) 29 June 2026 Procurement Officer (P2) 29 June 2026 Apply Here: https://www.nepad.org/work-with-us/careers If you aspire to work for a respected Pan-African organization driving sustainable development across Africa, these AUDA-NEPAD career opportunities could be the next step in your professional journey. Whether your expertise lies in finance, procurement, programme management, public health, or water and sanitation, these vacancies offer a platform to contribute to Africa s future while advancing your career. Apply before the respective deadlines and share these opportunities with colleagues, friends, and professionals who may be interested. title: CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Applications Open: Leadership Fellowship Opportunities for Students Through At-Large and Innovation Fellowship Pathways
summary: Apply for the Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Students interested in leadership development, public policy, government, civic engagement, innovation, and national affairs now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the most respected student leadership programs in the United States. The Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) has officially opened applications for the 2026–2027 Presidential Fellows Program, offering multiple pathways for students to participate. The Presidential Fellows Program is designed to cultivate the next generation of leaders by providing students with opportunities to engage deeply with issues related to the American presidency, Congress, public service, policymaking, and national leadership. Through a combination of research, collaboration, discussion, and practical engagement, fellows gain valuable experience that prepares them for future careers in government, public affairs, academia, law, technology, business, and nonprofit leadership. For the 2026–2027 cohort, CSPC is offering two distinct pathways for students who wish to become Presidential Fellows: The At-Large Program The Innovation Fellowship Applications are currently open and will close on July 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST. About the Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) The Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress is a nonpartisan educational institution dedicated to promoting a deeper understanding of the U.S. presidency, Congress, and public leadership. For decades, CSPC has brought together scholars, policymakers, students, and practitioners to examine the challenges facing democratic institutions and to foster informed civic participation. Through research initiatives, publications, leadership programs, and fellowships, the organization seeks to prepare future leaders to address complex policy challenges. The Presidential Fellows Program serves as one of CSPC’s flagship initiatives, helping students gain firsthand exposure to leadership concepts, policymaking processes, and contemporary governance issues. What Is the Presidential Fellows Program? The Presidential Fellows Program is a highly regarded leadership and educational initiative that brings together talented students from diverse academic disciplines and backgrounds. The program encourages participants to: Explore issues related to government and public policy. Engage in discussions on leadership and governance. Develop research and analytical skills. Collaborate with peers and experts. Gain exposure to real-world policy challenges. Strengthen civic engagement and public service values. The fellowship is designed to prepare students for leadership roles across a variety of sectors, including: Government Public policy International affairs Law Technology Science Business Academia Nonprofit organizations Two Pathways to Become a Presidential Fellow To ensure broader participation and inclusivity, CSPC offers two different pathways for students to join the Presidential Fellows Program. 1. At-Large Program The At-Large Program is specifically designed for students who wish to become Presidential Fellows but do not attend a CSPC partner institution. This pathway allows qualified students from colleges and universities across the country to participate in the fellowship regardless of whether their institution has a formal partnership with CSPC. Benefits of the At-Large Program Students accepted through this pathway can: Join the Presidential Fellows community. Participate in fellowship activities. Engage in leadership discussions. Connect with students from diverse institutions. Gain exposure to policy and governance topics. Build professional networks. This pathway expands access to students who might otherwise be unable to participate through institutional partnerships. 2. Innovation Fellowship The Innovation Fellowship is designed for students with backgrounds in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM). Recognizing the increasing intersection between technology, innovation, and public policy, this fellowship pathway seeks students who can contribute technical expertise and innovative thinking to discussions about leadership and governance. Who Should Apply? The Innovation Fellowship is ideal for students studying: Engineering Computer Science Data Science Artificial Intelligence Mathematics Physics Biological Sciences Technology-related disciplines The program encourages STEM students to explore how their technical knowledge can help shape public policy, solve societal challenges, and influence future leadership decisions. Benefits of the Innovation Fellowship Participants can: Connect technology with policy. Explore innovation-driven leadership. Engage in interdisciplinary discussions. Build networks with policymakers and scholars. Contribute STEM perspectives to governance issues. Why Apply for the Presidential Fellows Program? The CSPC Presidential Fellows Program offers a range of valuable benefits for students seeking leadership development and policy experience. Leadership Development Participants gain opportunities to: Strengthen leadership skills. Improve critical thinking abilities. Develop public speaking and communication skills. Build confidence in professional settings. Exposure to Public Policy Students gain deeper insight into: Government institutions. Public policy challenges. Legislative processes. Executive leadership. Democratic governance. Networking Opportunities Fellows can build meaningful relationships with: Fellow students. Policy professionals. Academics. Government leaders. Public service practitioners. Career Development Participation can strengthen applications for: Graduate schools. Public policy programs. Law schools. Government positions. Research opportunities. Leadership roles. Interdisciplinary Learning The program encourages collaboration among students from various fields, fostering broader perspectives and innovative approaches to solving public challenges. Application Deadline All applications for the 2026–2027 Presidential Fellows cohort must be submitted by: July 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST Interested students are encouraged to begin their applications early and carefully review all requirements before submission. How to Apply Applicants can choose the pathway that best aligns with their academic background and interests: At-Large Program For students who do not attend a partner institution. Innovation Fellowship For students with STEM backgrounds interested in leadership, governance, and public policy. Applicants should review eligibility requirements and submit their materials through the appropriate application portal before the deadline. Apply Here Final Thoughts The CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for students seeking to develop leadership skills, expand their understanding of public policy, and engage with pressing governance challenges. Whether applying through the At-Large Program or the Innovation Fellowship, participants will join a dynamic network of future leaders committed to making meaningful contributions to society. Students interested in leadership, public service, innovation, and civic engagement should strongly consider applying before the July 1, 2026 deadline to become part of the next generation of Presidential Fellows. For more information on this fellowship opportunity, visit here Explore more fellowship opportunities on OFY, click here The post CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Applications Open: Leadership Fellowship Opportunities for Students Through At-Large and Innovation Fellowship Pathways appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 Volunteer Opportunity in Morocco: Gain Hands-On Experience at One of Africa’s Premier Economic Events
summary: Applications Open for Volunteers at the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 in Rabat, Morocco The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) has officially launched a call for volunteers to support the 4th edition of the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026, one of the continent’s leading platforms for economic dialogue, policy discussions, and intellectual exchange. Scheduled to take place in Rabat, Morocco, from July 13–14, 2026, under the theme Turning Transitions into Growth, the symposium will bring together leading economists, policymakers, academics, researchers, development practitioners, and thought leaders from across Africa and around the world. This volunteer opportunity offers students and young professionals a unique chance to gain firsthand experience in organizing a high-level international conference while networking with some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s economic future. About the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) Since its establishment, the Africa Economic Symposium has evolved into a highly respected platform that bridges global perspectives with African realities. The symposium creates a space where: Rigorous research informs policy decisions. Economic experts engage with emerging African challenges. Policymakers exchange innovative ideas and solutions. Academics and practitioners collaborate on development strategies. Young professionals gain exposure to continental economic discussions. \Now in its fourth edition, AES has significantly expanded its influence and reach. AES 2026 by the Numbers The symposium is expected to: Host over 200 participants. Bring together economists, policymakers, and academics. Welcome representatives from more than 40 nationalities. Facilitate discussions on economic transformation across Africa. Provide a platform for evidence-based policy dialogue. The 2026 theme, Turning Transitions into Growth, will explore how African countries can transform economic, technological, demographic, environmental, and governance transitions into sustainable development opportunities. Why Volunteer for AES 2026? Volunteers will become an integral part of the symposium s operational and organizational success. Working closely with the Policy Center for the New South team, selected volunteers will gain practical experience in: 1. International Event Management Volunteers will participate in: Conference planning support. Event logistics coordination. Participant management. Venue operations. Registration processes. Session support. 2. Professional Networking This opportunity allows volunteers to interact with: Senior policymakers. International economists. Researchers. Development experts. Government officials. Academic leaders. 3. Communication and Media Exposure Volunteers may assist with: Media coordination. Communication activities. Information dissemination. Stakeholder engagement. Event documentation. 4. Practical Learning Experience Participants will gain valuable skills in: Teamwork. Leadership. Communication. Problem-solving. Project coordination. Event operations. 5. Access to the Symposium Volunteers will have opportunities to participate in selected portions of the official conference program, allowing them to learn directly from high-level discussions and presentations. Volunteer Responsibilities Selected volunteers will support the Policy Center for the New South before and during the symposium. Responsibilities may include: Logistical Support Registration assistance. Guest reception. Venue preparation. Coordination of conference materials. Participant guidance. Production Support Session setup. Speaker coordination. Technical support assistance. Event flow management. Communication and Media Tasks Information dissemination. Media coordination support. Participant communication. Event documentation. Administrative Duties Organizational support. Coordination activities. Documentation management. General conference assistance. Volunteers will work closely with PCNS staff throughout the preparation and implementation phases of the symposium. Volunteer Commitment Successful candidates must: Attend all orientation sessions. Participate in mandatory team meetings. Be available throughout the symposium period. Demonstrate professionalism and reliability. Maintain punctuality. Support the conference team as required. Volunteer Duration Earliest Start Date: July 12, 2026 Event Dates: July 13–14, 2026 Although the event itself spans two days, volunteers will begin preparations on July 12. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following criteria: Education Candidates should be: Bachelor s students. Master s students. Recent graduates. Young professionals. Language Requirements Applicants must have: Fluency in English. Preferred: French language proficiency. Arabic language proficiency. Additional Skills Successful applicants should demonstrate: Excellent communication skills. Strong interest in African economic issues. Flexibility and adaptability. Ability to work under pressure. Administrative and organizational capabilities. Professional conduct. Preferred Qualifications While not mandatory, experience in the following areas is advantageous: Event management. Conference coordination. Public relations. Communications. Development programs. Research support. Benefits of Participating Volunteers will enjoy several benefits, including: Professional Development Exposure to international conferences. Practical event management experience. Enhanced communication skills. Expanded professional network. Learning Opportunities Access to discussions on African economic development. Exposure to leading experts and policymakers. Insights into public policy and economic research. Networking Engagement with international delegates. Connections with academics and practitioners. Opportunities to build future career relationships. Event Support Benefits Meals provided throughout the event. Accommodation available when needed. Orientation and guidance from experienced professionals. Application Process Interested applicants must submit: Updated Resume/CV. Cover Letter. Applicants should ensure that both documents include a valid phone number, as interviews will be conducted via phone. Application Deadline June 26, 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. Interview Process Interviews are scheduled to begin from: July 1, 2026 Shortlisted candidates will be contacted directly. Apply Here About the Policy Center for the New South The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) is a leading Moroccan think tank dedicated to generating knowledge and policy solutions for Africa and the Global South. Based at the Rabat Campus of Mohammed VI Polytechnic University, PCNS conducts research and dialogue across several thematic areas including: Economy. Agriculture. International Relations. Security and Defense. Energy. Society. Sustainable Development. Through initiatives such as the Africa Economic Symposium, the organization fosters informed policy discussions that contribute to Africa’s development and economic transformation. Final Thoughts For students and young professionals interested in economics, public policy, development, and international affairs, volunteering at the Africa Economic Symposium 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity to gain real-world experience while contributing to one of Africa’s most influential economic gatherings. Beyond operational experience, volunteers will gain valuable exposure to policy discussions shaping the continent’s future, interact with global experts, and strengthen their professional networks. If you are passionate about Africa’s economic development and eager to learn from leading thinkers and decision-makers, this opportunity should not be missed. For more information on this opportunity visit here For more opportunities on OFY, click here The post Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 Volunteer Opportunity in Morocco: Gain Hands-On Experience at One of Africa’s Premier Economic Events appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026: Empowering Women Agripreneurs Across Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific
summary: Applications Open for the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026 Women entrepreneurs and aspiring agribusiness leaders across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their skills, expand their networks, and accelerate their agribusiness ventures through the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program. The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program is an initiative of the Agribusiness e-Academy under the Agribusiness Facility for Africa (ABF). The program is specifically designed to empower women who are actively engaged in agribusiness as well as those seeking to explore opportunities within the rapidly growing agrifood sector. Through structured online learning, mentorship opportunities, practical exposure, and market-oriented knowledge, the accelerator aims to equip women with the skills, confidence, and connections needed to thrive in today s evolving agricultural economy. About the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program Agriculture remains one of the most important economic sectors across many developing regions, yet women often face significant barriers in accessing training, resources, financing, markets, and business development opportunities. The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program seeks to bridge these gaps by providing women with access to quality agribusiness education and practical entrepreneurial support. The initiative is delivered through the Agribusiness e-Academy and leverages digital learning platforms to ensure broader accessibility for participants regardless of their location. The program welcomes: Women already operating agribusinesses Women involved in agricultural value chains Women-led agricultural enterprises Aspiring agripreneurs Young women interested in agrifood entrepreneurship Women seeking to build careers within the agricultural sector Program Objectives The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program aims to: Strengthen women s participation in agribusiness. Enhance entrepreneurial and business management skills. Improve market access and business opportunities. Promote innovation within the agrifood sector. Foster peer learning and networking. Provide mentorship and professional guidance. Build a pipeline of women leaders in agriculture and agribusiness. Support sustainable and inclusive agricultural development across participating regions. What Participants Can Expect Selected participants will benefit from a comprehensive accelerator experience that combines learning, mentorship, and practical engagement. Structured Learning Participants will engage in carefully designed learning modules covering key agribusiness topics. The structured approach ensures that women can build practical knowledge applicable to their businesses or future ventures. Mentorship Opportunities The program provides opportunities for participants to learn from experts and experienced professionals within the agribusiness ecosystem. Mentorship can help participants navigate business challenges, identify opportunities, and strengthen leadership skills. Market Exposure Participants will gain valuable insights into market trends, agribusiness opportunities, and strategies for positioning their products and services more effectively within agricultural value chains. Digital Learning Flexibility The program accommodates participants from diverse locations through virtual learning methods, making it easier for women to participate regardless of geographical constraints. Networking and Community Building Participants will become part of a growing network of women agripreneurs, industry stakeholders, and development professionals working to advance agricultural transformation across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific. Program Duration The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program runs for 8 weeks . Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the full duration of the program and participate in scheduled virtual learning sessions. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program, applicants should: Be female. Reside in an eligible OACP country. Be engaged in agribusiness activities or interested in entering the agrifood sector. Be willing to participate in virtual learning activities. Have access to a learning device such as: Smartphone Laptop Tablet Shared device Have some level of internet access. Commit to participating throughout the entire 8-week program. Application Information Required Applicants will be asked to provide: Full name Age range Country of residence Phone number (WhatsApp preferred) Email address Preferred language (English or French) Agribusiness experience information Learning device details Internet accessibility information Availability for weekly virtual sessions Motivation for joining the program Expected outcomes and learning goals Commitment to complete the program Why Women Should Apply Women continue to play a critical role across agricultural value chains, from production and processing to marketing and distribution. However, access to specialized business development opportunities remains limited for many. The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program offers an opportunity to: Improve agribusiness management skills. Learn from industry experts. Expand professional networks. Gain exposure to market opportunities. Strengthen business resilience. Explore innovation in agriculture. Build confidence as an entrepreneur. Connect with other women leaders across multiple regions. Languages Available The program supports participants in: English French This bilingual approach helps ensure broader participation across eligible countries. Selection Process After applications are submitted, eligible candidates will be reviewed by the program team. Selected participants will be contacted through: Email WhatsApp Successful applicants will receive further information regarding onboarding, learning schedules, and participation requirements. How to Apply Interested women should complete the official application form and provide all required information accurately. Applicants should clearly explain: Why they want to join the program. Their current involvement in agribusiness. Their future goals within the agrifood sector. What they hope to gain from participating. Providing thoughtful and detailed responses may help strengthen an application. Apply Here Final Thoughts The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program represents a significant opportunity for women across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific to gain practical knowledge, build professional networks, and strengthen their capacity to succeed in agribusiness. By combining structured learning, mentorship, and market exposure, the program seeks to empower a new generation of women agripreneurs capable of driving innovation, economic growth, food security, and sustainable development within their communities. Women interested in growing their agribusiness ventures or exploring opportunities within the agrifood sector are encouraged to submit their applications and take advantage of this transformative accelerator experience. For more information about this opportunity and to apply, click here . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026: Empowering Women Agripreneurs Across Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary
summary: Professionals passionate about expanding educational access, improving workforce training outcomes, and creating pathways to economic mobility now have an exciting opportunity to join Ascendium, a leading philanthropic organization committed to helping learners from low-income backgrounds succeed in postsecondary education and workforce training. Ascendium is currently recruiting for multiple Senior Program Officer positions to help lead and execute strategic grantmaking portfolios focused on expanding opportunity, supporting learner success, and connecting and aligning systems across the education and workforce sectors. These positions offer experienced professionals the chance to influence national education and workforce development initiatives, collaborate with leading organizations, fund innovative solutions, and generate knowledge that improves outcomes for underserved learners across the United States. Job Overview The Senior Program Officer serves as a lead strategy execution officer within one of Ascendium’s core philanthropic strategy teams: Expand Opportunity Support Learner Success Connect and Align Systems Reporting directly to a Strategy Director, successful candidates will play a major role in designing, implementing, managing, and evaluating strategic grantmaking portfolios that align with Ascendium’s mission and philanthropic goals. The role combines strategic leadership, grantmaking expertise, partnership development, policy engagement, learning and impact evaluation, and field-building activities. Salary and Compensation Ascendium plans to offer an annual salary of approximately: $125,000 per year Candidates with significant experience and qualifications may be eligible for higher compensation. In addition to a competitive salary, Ascendium offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support employee wellbeing, financial security, and professional growth. Location and Work Arrangement The position is primarily based in: Madison, Wisconsin, United States Ascendium operates a hybrid work model that includes: Three days per week working in the office. Two days per week working remotely. Candidates are expected to reside in or relocate to Madison, Wisconsin. However, Ascendium may consider remote candidates if qualified Madison-based applicants cannot be identified. Travel Requirements Successful candidates should be prepared to travel approximately: 6–10 times per year Travel may include: National conferences Strategic convenings Grant partner events Site visits Home weeks Other mission-aligned activities Areas of Expertise Being Sought Ascendium is hiring multiple Senior Program Officers and applicants should possess expertise in at least one of the following areas. 1. Workforce Reentry Ecosystems Experience involving: Workforce training Reentry supports Justice-impacted populations Local workforce systems Regional workforce partnerships National workforce organizations 2. Community College Transformation Expertise related to: Guided pathways Developmental education reform Institutional transformation Student success redesign Community college system improvement Postsecondary completion strategies 3. State-Level Cross-Sector Systems Alignment Experience involving: Postsecondary education systems Workforce development systems Corrections systems State policy implementation Data infrastructure development Dual enrollment programs Credit mobility initiatives 4. External Affairs Knowledge and experience in: State policy analysis Federal policy analysis Coalition building Strategic communications Public affairs Stakeholder engagement Field leadership Applicants are encouraged to clearly identify their area or areas of expertise in their cover letter. Key Responsibilities Senior Program Officers will have a broad range of responsibilities aimed at advancing Ascendium’s strategic objectives. Strategic Portfolio Leadership Responsibilities include: Designing strategic grantmaking portfolios. Managing grantmaking initiatives. Reviewing portfolio performance. Aligning grant investments with organizational goals. Developing responsive grantmaking priorities. Grantmaking and Funding Management Successful candidates will: Source ideas and potential partners. Assess strategic alignment of proposals. Guide applicants through the grant process. Develop funding recommendations. Draft funding proposals for board review. Structure reporting and payment processes. Learning and Impact Development Working closely with Senior Learning and Impact Officers, Senior Program Officers will: Develop learning agendas. Monitor strategic outcomes. Evaluate grant effectiveness. Identify lessons from funded initiatives. Improve future grantmaking strategies. Partner Support and Relationship Building The role includes: Supporting grant partners. Facilitating connections among organizations. Building collaborative networks. Strengthening partnerships with peer funders. Promoting knowledge sharing across the field. Convenings and Field Leadership Senior Program Officers will also: Lead strategic events and convenings. Facilitate discussions among stakeholders. Present at conferences. Participate in national working groups. Engage with professional associations. Contribute thought leadership pieces and publications. Team Collaboration and Mentorship Additional responsibilities include: Collaborating with colleagues across strategy teams. Mentoring Program Officers and Associate Program Officers. Supporting organizational planning and budgeting. Coordinating strategic communications efforts. Required Qualifications Candidates should possess a strong combination of education, leadership experience, and subject matter expertise. Educational Requirements Applicants should have knowledge equivalent to a master’s degree in fields such as: Education Philanthropy Social Sciences Social Work Community Development Economics Business Related disciplines Professional Experience Candidates should have: At least seven years of relevant experience. Experience in postsecondary education. Workforce development or workforce training experience. Philanthropic sector experience. Nonprofit leadership experience. Local, state, or federal government experience. Essential Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Commitment to equitable opportunity. Strong project management skills. Strategic thinking abilities. Stakeholder engagement expertise. Relationship-building skills. Leadership experience. Excellent written communication skills. Excellent verbal communication skills. Adaptability and problem-solving abilities. Ability to work collaboratively across sectors. Benefits Package Ascendium offers a highly competitive employee benefits package that includes: Health Coverage Medical insurance Dental insurance Vision insurance Family Support Up to eight weeks of paid parental leave Education Benefits Student loan repayment assistance Tuition reimbursement opportunities Retirement Planning Retirement savings plan Employer matching contributions These benefits are designed to help employees achieve long-term personal and professional success. Equal Opportunity Employment Ascendium is committed to equal employment opportunity and welcomes applications from qualified individuals regardless of: Race Color Religion Sex Age National origin Disability status Veteran status Other protected characteristics The organization actively promotes diversity, inclusion, and equitable opportunity in both its workplace and philanthropic initiatives. Important Immigration Information Applicants should note that: H-1B sponsorship, including initiation or transfer, is not available for this position. Candidates should ensure they have appropriate authorization to work in the United States before applying. Click here for more and to apply Why This Opportunity Stands Out The Senior Program Officer role at Ascendium offers far more than a traditional nonprofit or philanthropic position. It provides an opportunity to influence national systems, improve educational outcomes, shape workforce development initiatives, and create meaningful change for learners from low-income backgrounds. Professionals with expertise in education, workforce development, policy, philanthropy, community college reform, or systems transformation will find this role especially attractive due to its strategic impact, leadership opportunities, competitive compensation, and mission-driven focus. For experienced professionals looking to drive large-scale change in education and workforce systems, this opportunity represents a unique chance to make a lasting impact while advancing a rewarding career in philanthropy. click here for more global job opportunities The post Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
